sources.notes: Construct a sequence whose adjacent gcd values are all distinct.
The hard-version editorial gives the general construction by precomputed walks over specially chosen numbers.

annotation.summary: Construct n integers such that the gcd of every adjacent pair is distinct.
annotation.constraints: Easy version has smaller n, so a direct constructive graph walk over a small set of base numbers is enough.
annotation.core_idea: Choose numbers whose prime-exponent patterns make gcds identify edges between chosen vertices. Then output numbers along a walk that does not repeat an edge, so every adjacent gcd is unique. For the easy version, a small precomputed construction or manual Euler-trail style walk over a small graph suffices.
annotation.complexity: O(n) after precomputing a small walk.
tricks: make adjacent gcd identify the traversed edge

solution_variants.name: small-euler-walk-construction
solution_variants.summary: Use a small finite set of constructed numbers and output them along a walk whose edge gcds are unique.

tags.evidence: The task is solved by explicitly constructing a valid integer sequence rather than optimizing or testing.
The condition is entirely about making all adjacent gcd values distinct.
The construction is naturally viewed as a walk using graph edges at most once so each edge-coded gcd is unique.
tags.description: Euler trail/path style constructions or checks where edges must be used exactly once or at most once in a graph walk.
tags.created_reason: Euler-walk constructions are a standard graph technique and useful to retrieve distinct-edge/gcd construction problems.
